article_meta.title: Senate Democrats renew government shutdown threat as tensions flare with Trump by Alexander Bolton 12/22/25 06:00 AM ET

reasoning: The model commits multiple attribution rule violations by flagging quoted/attributed source language as author bias. Items 1 and 2 are both from attributed Democratic senators (one anonymous, one quoted directly). The model also uses non-standard bias type labels ('Loaded Language', 'Framing Bias', 'Lack of Balance') instead of the defined taxonomy. Item 3 about the previous shutdown being framed as a Democratic success is a defensible author-prose bias finding, though the label is wrong. Item 4 is partially defensible as a structural slant observation but uses a wrong label. The model misses some author-level bias patterns like 'another bombshell' and 'tensions flare' in the headline.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 1 and 2 are clear attribution rule violations. Item 1 flags language from 'A second Democratic senator who requested anonymity' - this is attributed source language. Item 2 flags a direct quote from a Democratic senator in quotation marks. These are serious violations of the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 1 and 2 should not have been flagged at all as they are attributed/quoted source language. Item 3 is a defensible observation about author framing. Item 4 is partially defensible but somewhat overreaching - the article does include Republican perspectives (Hoeven, Murkowski, anonymous GOP senator). So roughly half the flagged items are false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several author-prose bias instances: 'another bombshell that came from the White House' (Sensationalism), 'tensions flare' in headline (Word Choice), 'a serious affront to Democrats' in author prose (though this may be attributed), 'jam Senate Democrats again' (Word Choice/Spin). The framing of the previous shutdown as political success (item 3) was caught but several other instances were missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The model uses labels not in the defined taxonomy: 'Loaded Language' (should be Word Choice or Sensationalism/Emotionalism), 'Framing Bias' (should be Slant or Spin), 'Lack of Balance' (should be Slant or Bias by Omission). None of the four labels used match the provided taxonomy exactly.
